entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A legume crop whose seed production and varietal replacement are discussed in the context of developing countries.

entities.name: VARIETAL SEED REPLACEMENT RATE
entities.description: The rate at which improved seed varieties replace older ones in a crop.

entities.name: DEVELOPING COUNTRIES
entities.description: Geographic regions where seed replacement rates and crop productivity for groundnut remain low.

entities.name: VARIETAL IMPROVEMENT RESEARCH
entities.description: Scientific research aimed at improving crop varieties to increase benefits for farmers.

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of crop output, which remains low in developing countries for groundnut.

entities.name: SEED MULTIPLICATION RATIO
entities.description: The ratio of seeds produced to seeds planted, which is low for groundnut.

entities.name: SEED VIABILITY
entities.description: The ability of seeds to remain viable and germinate, which is lost quickly in groundnut.

entities.name: SELF POLLINATION
entities.description: The biological nature of groundnut to pollinate itself.

entities.name: PROFITABILITY
entities.description: The financial return from the seed production system, which is low for groundnut.

entities.name: SEED PRODUCTION SYSTEM
entities.description: The formal and informal processes used to produce seeds of improved varieties.

entities.name: COMMERCIAL SEED MULTIPLICATION
entities.description: The process of multiplying seeds for commercial purposes, discouraged by several factors in groundnut.

entities.name: CROP HUSBANDRY
entities.description: The practices of cultivating and managing the crop to enable seed production.
